nodes.label: Streaming Contract Documentation
nodes.rationale: infer_stream vs infer determines streaming; ProviderEvent::Complete is success-only; Error terminates without Complete.

nodes.label: Chat Completions Stream Assembly Fix Design
nodes.norm_label: chat completions stream assembly fix design

nodes.label: Chat Completions Stream Assembly Fix Proposal
nodes.norm_label: chat completions stream assembly fix proposal

nodes.label: Chat Completions Stream Assembly Fix Tasks
nodes.norm_label: chat completions stream assembly fix tasks

nodes.label: Indexed Stream Assembler State Model
nodes.rationale: Separate transport parsing from semantic assembly; key state by choice.index and tool_call.index to match provider-native schema and prevent cross-choice/cross-tool state corruption.

nodes.label: Semantic Completion Marker Strategy
nodes.rationale: Use finish_reason as primary completion boundary per choice, with [DONE] as final safety flush. Process all deltas in a chunk before flushing.

nodes.label: Completed-Only ProviderEvent::ToolCall Policy
nodes.rationale: ProviderEvent::ToolCall must only be emitted when a tool call is semantically complete. Arguments parsed once at flush time; fallback to raw string if JSON invalid.

nodes.label: Incremental Text Output Independence
nodes.rationale: Text deltas emit ProviderEvent::Output immediately regardless of pending tool-call assembly state to preserve low-latency streaming.

nodes.label: Tool Call Index Order Emission
nodes.rationale: Finalized tool calls emitted in ascending tool-call index order before ProviderEvent::Complete for deterministic regression tests.

nodes.label: Best-Effort Metadata Finalization
nodes.rationale: If provider omits/delays tool-call id or name, preserve latest non-empty values and emit best-effort completed event rather than failing the stream.

nodes.label: Compile-Time Markdown Fragment Inclusion
nodes.rationale: Store fragments as Markdown files under src/system_prompt_fragments/ included with include_str! for allocation-free static access without runtime I/O.

nodes.label: ApiFamily-Based Fragment Resolution
nodes.rationale: Map prompt fragments by ApiFamily rather than provider slug so all compatible providers share guidance without per-slug branches.

nodes.label: Credential Kind vs Wire Auth Strategy Separation
nodes.rationale: CredentialKind (ApiKey/OAuthBearer) and AuthStrategy (BearerToken/ApiKeyHeader) are separate axes. kimi-code proves they differ: API key uses x-api-key, OAuth uses Bearer token.
nodes.norm_label: credential kind vs wire auth strategy separation

nodes.label: Codex as Separate Provider from OpenAI
nodes.rationale: Codex uses ChatGPT/Codex backend endpoints, not public OpenAI API. Separate ApiFamily::CodexResponses keeps exact request requirements testable without bending existing OpenAI adapters.
nodes.norm_label: codex as separate provider from openai

nodes.label: Unverified JWT Routing Metadata Extraction
nodes.rationale: Parse JWT payloads unverified for chatgpt_account_id routing header only; provider servers still validate bearer tokens. Claim priority: top-level chatgpt_account_id > nested https://api.openai.com/auth.chatgpt_account_id > organizations[].id.

nodes.label: OAuth Refresh Lifecycle Ownership Boundary
nodes.rationale: iron-providers receives credential snapshots from iron-core/application but does not own OAuth refresh tokens or flows. ProviderCredential has no refresh_token field.
